channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: What changes to the CHIPS act could mean for AI growth, consumers
item.description: Since taking office, Trump has said he would place tariffs on foreign production of computer chips and semiconductors in order to return chip manufacturing to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: OpenAI board unanimously rejects Elon Musk's $97.4 billion proposal
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — OpenAI says its board of directors has unanimously rejected a $97.4 billion takeover bid by Elon Musk. “OpenAI is not for sale, and the board has unanimously rejected Mr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday updates: Keeping up with the latest fixes
item.description: Long before Taco Tuesday became part of the pop-culture vernacular, Tuesdays were synonymous with security — and for anyone in the tech world, they still are.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: For February’s Patch Tuesday, Microsoft rolls out 63 updates
item.description: Microsoft released 63 patches for Windows, Microsoft Office, and developer platforms in this week’s Patch Tuesday update. The February release was a relatively light update, but it comes with significant testing requirements for networking and remote desktop environments.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Worrying YouTube security flaw exposed billions of user emails
item.description: Experts find a simple flaw could have exposed any email attached to a YouTube account until recently.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Big Tech Ditches Nvidia: OpenAI's First Custom AI Chip Ready for Mass Production
item.description: OpenAI is on the verge of launching its first custom AI chip, a move that could change the game for the tech giant and make it less reliant on Nvidia.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Unlocking AI’s full potential in procurement with smarter data management
item.description: Supply chain operations are often stymied by inefficiencies that result in higher costs, longer lead times, and dissatisfied customers.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Apple and the big store
item.description: Apple has made a small but significant move by introducing its Apple TV app to Android. It might seem like a minor step, but it marks a major pivot in strategy as the company expands its services beyond its own ecosystem.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Italian government denies it spied on journalists and migrant activists using Paragon spyware
item.description: ROME (AP) — The Italian government denied Wednesday that it had spied on journalists and migrant activists using spyware but said it would cooperate with an investigation into “vulnerabilities” after at least seven Italian cellphones were apparently hacked with military-grade surveillance technology
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and PAOLO SANTALUCIA, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: OpenAI revamps AI roadmap, merging models for a leaner future
item.description: OpenAI will integrate “o3” into GPT-5 instead of releasing it separately, streamlining adoption while signaling a shift toward fewer, more controlled AI models amid rising competition and cost pressures.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Go 1.24 arrives with generic type aliases, boosted WebAssembly support
item.description: Go 1.24, an update to Google‘s popular open source programming language, is now generally available as a production release, with full backing for generic type aliases, performance improvements, and improved WebAssembly support. The release was unveiled February 11, and can be downloaded from Go.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: BBC: Chatbots distort the facts about news
item.description: It’s already known that today’s generative AI (genAI) tools often have trouble with basic facts. Now, it’s clear they don’t well with current events either.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to not fall in love with AI-powered romance scammers
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explains how to protect yourself against artificial intelligence-fueled romance scams, which have become the most common type of fraud in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: OSINT gets its own subcommittee on House intelligence panel
item.description: The intelligence community has increasingly prioritized OSINT in recent years. Now, House lawmakers are creating a new subcommittee dedicated to the discipline. The post OSINT gets its own subcommittee on House intelligence panel first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: IP Fabric expands network visibility, automation with platform update
item.description: As modern networking deployments across the cloud and edge become increasingly complex, it can be a non-trivial exercise for teams to get proper visibility.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Don't fall for that sneaky iCloud storage alert in your inbox
item.description: Beware of iCloud scams using urgent messages. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson helps you spot the red flags to protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why the generative AI hype is good
item.description: Elon Musk predicts artificial general intelligence (AGI) by 2025. Former OpenAI employee Leopold Aschenbrenner says AGI by 2027. Futurist Ray Kurzweil bets on 2029 for AI surpassing human intelligence. OpenAI cofounder Ilya Sutskever declared that language models are “slightly conscious.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: ESA’s space telescope Euclid discovers ‘astonishing' Einstein ring
item.description: The European Space Agency's Euclid space telescope was going through preliminary tests in September 2023 when it captured images of an Einstein Ring in a nearby galaxy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: News influencers favored Trump over Harris during campaign, study finds
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Americans who followed news influencers during the presidential campaign were more likely to hear positive reports about Donald Trump than they were about Kamala Harris, a study has revealed.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The future is far from doom and gloom for DBAs
item.description: Database administration has always been something of a well kept secret. Even within the wider domain of information technology, database administration is one of those areas of expertise that often goes overlooked by aspiring STEM students and tech-savvy job seekers.
item.dc:creator: Info World
